title: Properties of magnetic force on charged particle
description: Let's solve some problems to better understand some of the interesting properties of the force experienced by charged particle in a magnetic field.
topic_path: Properties of magnetic force on charged particle

assessment_items.question_content:
A particle undergoes circular motion in a uniform magnetic field. It is observed that the speed of the particle remains constant throughout the motion.

**Which of the following is the correct _reason_ for the particle having constant speed?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The force is perpendicular to the velocity at every point.
The force is perpendicular to the magnetic field at every point.
Magnetic force is not strong enough to change the speed.

assessment_items.question_content:
The kinetic energy of a particle is measured at various points of its motion in a uniform magnetic field. It is seen that the kinetic energy remains the same throughout the motion.

**Which of the following is the correct _reason_ for the particle having constant kinetic energy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The force is perpendicular to the velocity at every point.
The force is perpendicular to the magnetic field at every point.
Magnetic force is not strong enough to change the kinetic energy.

assessment_items.question_content:
A charged particle at _rest_ in a uniform magnetic field is shown below.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of these best describes the effect of _doubling_ the magnetic field strength on the force experienced by the particle?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The force on the charged particle doubles.
The force on the charged particle remains unchanged.
The force on the charged particle becomes half.

assessment_items.question_content:
A particle having charge $q$ is at _rest_ in a uniform magnetic field as shown below. 

[[☃ image 1]]

The charged particle is replaced by another one having charge $2q$.

**Which of these best describes the force on the new particle?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Double the force on the old particle.
Same as the force on the old particle.
Half the force on the old particle.

assessment_items.question_content:
A particle moving with velocity $\bold v$ in a magnetic field $\bold B$ experiences a magnetic force $\bold F$.

**Which of these shows possible directions of the three vectors $\bold v, \bold B$ and $\bold F$?**$\\$
_Note: $\redE{\theta}$ is an acute angle in the figures._

[[☃ radio 1]]

A particle moving with velocity $\bold v$ in a magnetic field $\bold B$ experiences a magnetic force $\bold F$.

**Which of these shows possible directions of the three vectors $\bold v, \bold B$ and $\bold F$?**$\\$
_Note: $\otimes$ represents the direction into the plane. $\redE{\theta}$ is an acute angle in the figures._

All three particles have the same speed, $|\bold v_1|=|\bold v_2|=|\bold v_3|=|\bold v|$.
